We spend all this time building AI agents that are great at talking. But 99 percent of the value is when they stop talking and actually do something. The biggest hurdle has always been connecting the AI brain to the real world tools, reliably. That just got solved. n8n just launched support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP). If you haven't heard of it, pay attention, because this changes how your AI agents operate. Think of it like this: Your AI agent is the CEO. Instead of trying to train the CEO on every complex task, the CEO now has a standardized way to "hire" an expert. That expert is your n8n workflow. When the AI needs to update a Stripe invoice, generate a PDF report, and push the results into Slack, it doesn’t fumble with APIs. It just tells the MCP standardized interface, "I need X done." And the pre-built n8n workflow executes the entire sequence perfectly. This is the bridge that turns clever conversational AI into an actual operational workforce. It standardizes the conversation between the AI model and the workflow builder. This is the difference between an intern who chats a lot and a team lead who delegates tasks and gets results. We’re using this right now with Lovable and Claude to handle complex data processing requests directly triggered by code interpretations. It is extremely powerful and cleanly implemented.
